assessments.status_rationale: Deadline is 2026-07-31 and the condition depends on Meta’s Q1 2026 and Q2 2026 headcount disclosures (10-Q or earnings release). Today’s news items do not provide the required headcount figures.
Deadline is 2026-12-31 and requires a completed U.S. IPO and proceeds verified in a final prospectus (424B). No IPO/prospectus evidence appears in today’s news bullets.

Deadline is 2026-10-31 and requires a binding federal directive defining “frontier” via explicit compute/capability thresholds plus pre-deployment testing/red-teaming requirements. Today’s bullets do not mention any AI-testing directive; the noted executive order is unrelated (psychedelic drugs).

Deadline is 2026-09-30 and requires a CISA announcement of a new vulnerability-context program with an API/feed covering ≥50% of newly published CVEs. No CISA/CVE program announcement appears in today’s items.

Deadline is 2026-12-31 and requires a publicly announced CFTC/DOJ enforcement action tied to manipulation/insider trading in crude/refined products during the April 2026 Iran conflict period. Today’s news includes conflict/oil-market coverage but no enforcement action announcement.

Deadline is 2026-06-30 and requires a major shipping analytics provider to publish that Hormuz crude-oil tanker transits returned to ≥90% of pre-conflict baseline on a 14-day rolling basis. Today’s reporting (e.g., CNBC/NYT) discusses renewed closure and risks, which does not satisfy the specific analytics-provider publication requirement.
Resolution depends on the 2026-09-16 FOMC statement relative to the target range in effect on 2026-04-18. No FOMC decision is reported in today’s bullets.

Deadline is 2026-12-31 and requires Canada to table federal legislation expanding national-security foreign-investment review in named strategic sectors, evidenced by a bill introduced plus a government release/backgrounder. No such Canadian legislative action is indicated in today’s news bullets.
